uncountable_nouns.title: Uncountable Nouns
uncountable_nouns.tip: 'Data', 'information', 'advice', and 'evidence' are uncountable nouns. They do not take an 's' and usually take a singular verb.

subject_verb_agreement.title: Subject-Verb Agreement
subject_verb_agreement.tip: Use 'does' or 'doesn\'t' with third-person singular subjects (he, she, it). Use 'do' or 'don\'t' with I, you, we, they.

first_conditional.title: First Conditional
first_conditional.tip: In first conditional sentences, use the Present Simple in the 'if' clause, not 'will'. (If + Present Simple, ... will + infinitive).

prepositions.title: Prepositions with 'Despite'
prepositions.tip: Use 'despite' without 'of'. You can say 'despite the rain' or 'in spite of the rain', but never 'despite of'.

gerunds_infinitives.title: Gerunds after Prepositions
gerunds_infinitives.tip: The phrase 'look forward to' ends with the preposition 'to'. Prepositions are followed by nouns or gerunds (verb+ing), not base verbs.

subject_verb_agreement_complex.title: Complex Subject-Verb Agreement
subject_verb_agreement_complex.tip: The phrase 'The number of [plural noun]' takes a singular verb. 'A number of [plural noun]' takes a plural verb.
